name: Koyo (Autumn Leaves)
traditions: Viewing momiji (maple) leaves at temples; nighttime illuminations; autumn kaiseki cuisine; tea ceremonies; photography pilgrimages.
historicalBackground: Koyo (autumn leaf viewing) dates to the Heian period. Kyoto's temples, especially Tōfuku-ji and Eikan-dō, are iconic autumn destinations.
description: Japan's magical autumn leaf season, when Kyoto's ancient temples are set ablaze in crimson and gold foliage.
